Since you're looking at upgrading from your Stratocaster to a Gibson Les Paul, the biggest shock to your system is going to be the physical feel. You specifically wanted to test out coil-splitting to get those acoustic-like fingerpicking tones for songs like "Blackbird." A lot of humbuckers sound thin and anemic when split, rather than giving you that crisp single-coil snap you're used to from your Strat. Make sure the split tone actually gives you the flexibility you're after.

Also, don't just play it in standard. Drop it into open D right there in the store. See how the humbuckers handle the lower register and whether the guitar holds its tuning with the altered tension. If the low strings get muddy in open D, it defeats the purpose of the upgrade.
